production: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland — Karaoke Reader (Demo)

passages.title: A Rabbit With a Watch
passages.text: ALICE WAS BORED BY THE RIVER. SUDDENLY A WHITE RABBIT RAN BY. IT HAD PINK EYES. “OH DEAR! OH DEAR!” IT SAID. “I SHALL BE LATE!” IT TOOK A WATCH FROM ITS WAISTCOAT. ALICE JUMPED UP AND RAN AFTER IT.
passages.regenerate_prompt: Classic children's-book watercolor: a white rabbit standing upright wearing a little checked waistcoat, holding a round gold pocket watch and looking worried; a curious young girl in a blue dress watching from behind a tree on a sunny riverbank; warm, gentle, John Tenniel-inspired but soft and colorful.

passages.title: Down, Down, Down
passages.text: DOWN WENT ALICE AFTER IT. THE RABBIT-HOLE WENT STRAIGHT DOWN. THEN IT DIPPED SUDDENLY DOWN. DOWN, DOWN, DOWN SHE FELL. WOULD THE FALL NEVER END? SHE FELL PAST CUPBOARDS AND SHELVES. SHE FELL VERY, VERY SLOWLY.
passages.regenerate_prompt: Storybook illustration: a young girl in a blue dress falling gently down a very deep round well, arms out, dress billowing; the curved walls are lined with little cupboards, bookshelves, jars and a map; warm lamplight, dreamy, slightly tumbling perspective.

passages.title: The Little Door
passages.text: ALICE CAME TO A LONG HALL. ALL ROUND WERE LOCKED DOORS. THEN SHE FOUND A TINY DOOR. IT WAS FIFTEEN INCHES HIGH. A LITTLE GOLDEN KEY OPENED IT. BEHIND IT WAS A LOVELY GARDEN. BUT ALICE WAS FAR TOO BIG. SHE COULD NOT EVEN FIT HER HEAD!
problems: The earlier plate drew the door HUGE, contradicting 'fifteen inches high'. Regenerated so the door is clearly tiny and Alice is enormous beside it.
passages.regenerate_prompt: Children's-book watercolor: a GIANT young girl in a blue dress kneeling and bending all the way down to peer through a TINY door only about fifteen inches high set in the base of a wall; the little door is unmistakably MUCH smaller than her head; through the small open doorway we glimpse a bright sunny garden of roses, fountains and hedges; a little golden key on the floor; warm, classic, whimsical. The door must look tiny and the girl must look huge.

passages.title: DRINK ME
passages.text: ON THE TABLE WAS A BOTTLE. A LABEL SAID: “DRINK ME.” ALICE TOOK A LITTLE SIP. IT TASTED LIKE CHERRY TART. “WHAT A CURIOUS FEELING!” SHE SAID. SHE WAS SHUTTING UP LIKE A TELESCOPE. NOW SHE WAS TEN INCHES HIGH!
passages.regenerate_prompt: Storybook watercolor: a little glass bottle with a paper label reading 'DRINK ME' tied with string, sitting on a three-legged glass table; a young girl in a blue dress shrinking small beside it, looking surprised; soft magical light.

passages.title: EAT ME
passages.text: UNDER THE TABLE WAS A CAKE. ON IT, CURRANTS SPELLED: “EAT ME.” “I’LL EAT IT,” SAID ALICE. SHE TOOK ONE LITTLE BITE. “WHICH WAY? WHICH WAY?” SHE ASKED. THEN UP, UP, UP SHE GREW. NOW SHE WAS NINE FEET TALL!
passages.regenerate_prompt: Storybook watercolor: a small round frosted cake on the floor with the words 'EAT ME' spelled in dark currants on top; a young girl in a blue dress suddenly grown very tall, her head near the ceiling, looking astonished; warm whimsical light.

passages.title: Curiouser and Curiouser
passages.text: “CURIOUSER AND CURIOUSER!” CRIED ALICE. SHE WAS SO SURPRISED. SHE FORGOT TO TALK PROPERLY. “NOW I’M OPENING OUT LIKE A TELESCOPE!” “GOODBYE, FEET!” SHE CALLED. HER FEET WERE SO FAR AWAY NOW. OH, WHAT A STRANGE DAY!
passages.regenerate_prompt: Whimsical storybook watercolor: a young girl in a blue dress grown impossibly tall, looking down and waving cheerfully at her own tiny feet far below; playful, warm, gentle humor, classic children's-book style.
